post.description:
Create Project

 Create a new project. A project is a vessel to group applications, roles and
 authorizations. Every project belongs to exactly one organization, but
 can be granted to other organizations for self-management of their authorizations.

 Required permission:
   - `project.create`

Update Project

 Update an existing project.

 Required permission:
   - `project.write`

Delete Project

 Delete an existing project.
 In case the project is not found, the request will return a successful response as
 the desired state is already achieved.

 Required permission:
   - `project.delete`

Get Project

 Returns the project identified by the requested ID.

 Required permission:
   - `project.read`

List Projects

 List all matching projects. By default all projects of the instance that the caller
 has permission to read are returned.
 Make sure to include a limit and sorting for pagination.

 Required permission:
   - `project.read`

Deactivate Project

 Set the state of a project to deactivated. Request returns no error if the project is already deactivated.
 Applications under deactivated projects are not able to login anymore.

 Required permission:
   - `project.write`

Activate Project

 Set the state of a project to active. Request returns no error if the project is already activated.

 Required permission:
   - `project.write`

Add Project Role

 Add a new project role to a project. The key must be unique within the project.

 Required permission:
   - `project.role.write`

Update Project Role

 Change a project role. The key is not editable. If a key should change, remove the role and create a new one.

 Required permission:
   - `project.role.write`

Remove Project Role

 Removes the role from the project and on every resource it has a dependency.
 This includes project grants and user grants (role assignments).

 Required permission:
   - `project.role.write`

List Project Roles

 Returns all roles of a project matching the search query.

 Required permission:
   - `project.role.read`

Create Project Grant

 Grant a project to another organization.
 The project grant will allow the granted organization to access the project and manage
 the authorizations for its users.

 Required permission:
   - `project.grant.create`

Update Project Grant

 Change the roles of the project that is granted to another organization.
 The project grant will allow the granted organization to access the project and manage
 the authorizations for its users.

 Required permission:
   - `project.grant.write`

Delete Project Grant

 Delete a project grant. All user grants (role assignments) for this project grant will also be removed.
 A user will not have access to the project afterward (if permissions are checked).
 In case the project grant is not found, the request will return a successful response as
 the desired state is already achieved.

 Required permission:
   - `project.grant.delete`

Deactivate Project Grant

 Set the state of the project grant to deactivated.
 Applications under deactivated projects grants are not able to login anymore.

 Required permission:
   - `project.grant.write`

Activate Project Grant

 Set the state of the project grant to activated.

 Required permission:
   - `project.grant.write`

List Project Grants

 Returns a list of project grants. A project grant is when the organization grants its project
 to another organization.

 Required permission:
   - `project.grant.read`

id.title: id
id.description: Only return resources that belong to this id.

ids.title: ids
ids.description: Defines the ids to query for.

offset.title: offset
offset.description: Starting point for retrieval, in combination of offset used to query a set list of objects.

limit.title: limit
limit.description:
limit is the maximum amount of objects returned. The default is set to 100
 with a maximum of 1000 in the runtime configuration.
 If the limit exceeds the maximum configured Zitadel will throw an error.
 If no limit is present the default is taken.

asc.title: asc
asc.description:
Asc is the sorting order. If true the list is sorted ascending, if false
 the list is sorted descending. The default is descending.

totalResult.title: total_result
totalResult.description: Absolute number of objects matching the query, regardless of applied limit.

appliedLimit.title: applied_limit
appliedLimit.description: Applied limit from query, defines maximum amount of objects per request, to compare if all objects are returned.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project.

grantedOrganizationId.title: granted_organization_id
grantedOrganizationId.description: GrantedOrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization the project was granted to.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the change of the project grant.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the change of the project.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project.

roleKey.title: role_key
roleKey.description:
RoleKey identifies the role. It's the only relevant attribute for Zitadel and
 will be used for authorization checks and as claim in tokens and user info responses.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description: DisplayName is a human readable name for the role, which might be displayed to users.

group.title: group
group.description:
Group allows grouping roles for display purposes. Zitadel will not handle it in any way.
 It can be used to group roles in a UI to allow easier management for administrators.
 This attribute is not to be confused with groups as a collection of users.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp of the project role creation.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project.

grantedOrganizationId.title: granted_organization_id
grantedOrganizationId.description: GrantedOrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization the project will be granted to.

roleKeys.title: role_keys
roleKeys.description:
RoleKeys is a list of roles to be granted to the organization for self management.
 The roles are identified by their keys.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp of the project grant creation.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization the project belongs to.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description:
ProjectID is the unique identifier of the new project. This field is optional.
 If omitted, the system will generate a unique ID for you. This is the
 recommended way. The generated ID will be returned in the response.

name.title: name
name.description: Name of the project. This might be presented to users, e.g. in sign-in flows.

projectRoleAssertion.title: project_role_assertion
projectRoleAssertion.description:
ProjectRoleAssertion is a  setting that can be enabled to have role information
 included in the user info endpoint.
 It is also dependent on your application settings to include it in tokens and other types.

authorizationRequired.title: authorization_required
authorizationRequired.description:
AuthorizationRequired is a boolean flag that can be enabled to check if a user has
 an authorization to use this project assigned when login into an application of this project.

projectAccessRequired.title: project_access_required
projectAccessRequired.description:
ProjectAccessRequired is a boolean flag that can be enabled to check if the organization
 of the user, that is trying to log in,
 has access to this project (either owns the project or is granted).

privateLabelingSetting.title: private_labeling_setting
privateLabelingSetting.description:
PrivateLabelingSetting is a setting that defines which private labeling/branding should
 trigger when getting to a login of this project.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description:
DeletionDate is the timestamp of the deletion of the project grant.
 Note that the deletion date is only guaranteed to be set if the deletion was successful during the request.
 In case the deletion occurred in a previous request, the deletion date might be empty.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project to be deleted.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description:
DeletionDate is the timestamp of the deletion of the project.
 Note that the deletion date is only guaranteed to be set if the deletion was successful during the request.
 In case the deletion occurred in a previous request, the deletion date might be empty.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project to be retrieved.

project.description: Project is the project that matches the project ID used in the request.

pagination.description: Pagination can be used to list limitations and ordering.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description:
SortingColumn is the field the result is sorted by. The default is the creation date.
 Beware that if you change this, your result pagination might be inconsistent.

name.description: Name is the name of the project. This might be presented to users, e.g. in sign-in flows.

state.description: State describes the current state of the project.

projectRoleAssertion.title: project_role_assertion
projectRoleAssertion.description: ProjectRoleAssertion is a boolean flag that describes if the roles of the user should be added to the token.

authorizationRequired.title: authorization_required
authorizationRequired.description:
AuthorizationRequired is a boolean flag that can be enabled to check if a user has
 an authorization to use this project assigned when login into an application of this project.

projectAccessRequired.title: project_access_required
projectAccessRequired.description:
ProjectAccessRequired is a boolean flag that can be enabled to check if the organization of the user,
 that is trying to log in, has access to this project (either owns the project or is granted).

privateLabelingSetting.title: private_labeling_setting
privateLabelingSetting.description: PrivateLabelingSetting defines from where the private labeling should be triggered.

grantedOrganizationId.title: granted_organization_id
grantedOrganizationId.description:
GrantedOrganizationID is the ID of the organization the project is granted to.
 In case the project is not granted, this field is unset.

grantedOrganizationName.title: granted_organization_name
grantedOrganizationName.description:
GrantedOrganizationName is the name of the organization the project is granted to.
 In case the project is not granted, this field is unset.

grantedState.title: granted_state
grantedState.description:
GrantedProjectState describes the current state of the granted project.
 In case the project is not granted, this field is set to GrantedProjectState.GRANTED_PROJECT_STATE_UNSPECIFIED.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: The unique identifier of the organization which granted the project to the granted_organization_id.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: The timestamp of the granted project creation.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: The timestamp of the last change to the granted project (e.g. creation, activation, deactivation).

grantedOrganizationId.title: granted_organization_id
grantedOrganizationId.description: The ID of the organization the project is granted to.

grantedOrganizationName.title: granted_organization_name
grantedOrganizationName.description: The name of the organization the project is granted to.

grantedRoleKeys.title: granted_role_keys
grantedRoleKeys.description: The roles granted to the organization for self-management of the project.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: The ID of the granted project.

projectName.title: project_name
projectName.description: The name of the granted project.

state.title: state
state.description: Describes the current state of the granted project.

grantedOrganizationIdFilter.description: Filter for project grants that were granted to a specific organization.

inProjectIdsFilter.title: in_project_ids_filter
inProjectIdsFilter.description: Filter for project grants of a specific project ID.

organizationIdFilter.title: organization_id_filter
organizationIdFilter.description: Filter for project grants that were granted from a specific organization.

projectNameFilter.title: project_name_filter
projectNameFilter.description: Filter for project grants with a specific project name.

roleKeyFilter.title: role_key_filter
roleKeyFilter.description: Filter for project grants where a specific role is granted.

projectName.title: project_name
projectName.description: Defines the name of the project to query for.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text comparison method used for the name query.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID Is the ID of the organization to query for.

type.title: type
type.description:
Defines whether to filter for owned, granted or both types of projects.
 If not specified, defaults to OWNED_OR_GRANTED and will return both owned and granted projects.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the ID of the project the role belongs to.

key.title: key
key.description:
Key of the project role. The key identifies the role. It's the only relevant attribute for Zitadel and
 will be used for authorization checks and as claim in tokens and user info responses.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp of the project role creation.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the last change to the project role.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description: DisplayName is a human readable name for the role, which might be displayed to users.

group.title: group
group.description:
Group allows grouping roles for display purposes. Zitadel will not handle it in any way.
 It can be used to group roles in a UI to allow easier management for administrators.
 This attribute is not to be confused with groups as a collection of users.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description: The display name of the project role to query for.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text comparison method used for the name query.

key.title: key
key.description: The key of the project role to query for.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text comparison method used for the key query.

displayNameFilter.title: display_name_filter
displayNameFilter.description: Filter for project roles with a specific display name.

roleKeyFilter.title: role_key_filter
roleKeyFilter.description: Filter for project roles with a specific key.

inProjectIdsFilter.title: in_project_ids_filter
inProjectIdsFilter.description: Filter for projects with a specific ID.

organizationIdFilter.title: organization_id_filter
organizationIdFilter.description:
Filter for projects that are owned by or granted to a specific organization.
 You can specify whether to search for owned, granted or both types of projects.

projectNameFilter.title: project_name_filter
projectNameFilter.description: Filter for projects with a specific name.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project.

roleKey.title: role_key
roleKey.description:
RoleKey is the key of the role to be removed.
 All dependencies of this role will be removed as well, including project grants and user grants (role assignments).
 If the role is not found, the request will return a successful response as the desired state is already achieved.

removalDate.title: removal_date
removalDate.description:
RemovalDate is the timestamp of the removal of the project role.
 Note that the removal date is only guaranteed to be set if the removal was successful during the request.
 In case the removal occurred in a previous request, the removal date might be empty.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project.

grantedOrganizationId.title: granted_organization_id
grantedOrganizationId.description: GrantedOrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization the project was granted to.

roleKeys.title: role_keys
roleKeys.description:
RoleKeys is a list of roles to be granted to the organization for self management.
 The roles are identified by their keys.
 Any roles not included in this list will be removed from the project grant.
 If you want to add a role, make sure to include all other existing roles as well.
 If any previous role is removed, all user grants (role assignments) for this project grant with this role will be removed as well.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the change of the project grant.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project to be updated.

name.title: name
name.description:
Name is used to update the name of the project. This field is optional.
 If omitted, the name will remain unchanged.

projectRoleAssertion.title: project_role_assertion
projectRoleAssertion.description:
ProjectRoleAssertion is a setting that can be enabled to have role information
 included in the user info endpoint.
 It is also dependent on your application settings to include it in tokens and other types.
 If omitted, the setting will remain unchanged.

authorizationRequired.title: authorization_required
authorizationRequired.description:
AuthorizationRequired is a boolean flag that can be enabled to check if a user has
 a role of this project assigned when logging into an application of this project.
 If omitted, the setting will remain unchanged.

projectAccessRequired.title: project_access_required
projectAccessRequired.description:
ProjectAccessRequired is a boolean flag that can be enabled to check if the organization
 of the user has a grant to this project.
 If omitted, the setting will remain unchanged.

privateLabelingSetting.title: private_labeling_setting
privateLabelingSetting.description:
PrivateLabelingSetting is a setting that defines which private labeling/branding should
 trigger when getting to a login of this project.
 If omitted, the setting will remain unchanged.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the change of the project.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project.

roleKey.title: role_key
roleKey.description:
RoleKey identifies the role. It's the only relevant attribute for Zitadel and
 will be used for authorization checks and as claim in tokens and user info responses.
 It cannot be changed. If you need a different key, remove the role and create a new one.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description:
DisplayName is the human readable name for the role, which might be displayed to users.
 If omitted, the name will remain unchanged.

group.title: group
group.description:
Group allows grouping roles for display purposes. Zitadel will not handle it in any way.
 It can be used to group roles in a UI to allow easier management for administrators.
 If omitted, the group will remain unchanged.
 This attribute is not to be confused with groups as a collection of users.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the change of the project role.

connect-protocol-version.title: Connect-Protocol-Version
connect-protocol-version.description: Define the version of the Connect protocol

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

google.protobuf.Any.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message.
